widgetMetadata.instructions: Each entry under `widgets` describes one chart/widget rendered on this page of the Israeli Ecosystem dashboard. Descriptions are result-agnostic: they explain what the visualization shows and how to interpret it, not the specific numbers. The `dataPaths` array on each entry lists dot-notation paths inside this same JSON file where the underlying data for that widget lives (e.g. "kpis.patentingStartups" or "ipIntensity.items") When summarizing or answering questions about a number from this snapshot, locate the relevant widget(s) by `dataPaths` and quote the corresponding `description` to explain the metric. Widgets that have a 10 year trend usually show the total overall (across all years) and you get the exact data for each year in the past 10 years. The 10 year data do not add up to the total of course

startups:map.title: Israel Startup Map
startups:map.description: Geographic distribution of Israeli startups by their registered address in Israel. Each city marker is sized by the number of startups with an active legal registration in that city and colored by its administrative district. The legend lists the same totals by district. Only startups with a known registered Israeli address appear.

startups:kpi-total-startups.title: Total Startups
startups:kpi-total-startups.description: Total number of Israeli startups in the dataset (active, exited, and closed combined). The bar chart shows new startups by founding year over the past 10 years.

startups:kpi-funding-rounds.title: Funding Rounds
startups:kpi-funding-rounds.description: Total number of disclosed funding rounds raised by Israeli startups across all time and stages. The bar chart shows the count of rounds closed each year over the past 10 years.

startups:kpi-total-raised.title: Total Raised
startups:kpi-total-raised.description: Total amount of capital (USD) raised by Israeli startups across all disclosed funding rounds. The bar chart shows the dollar amount raised each year over the past 10 years.

startups:kpi-acquisitions.title: Acquisitions
startups:kpi-acquisitions.description: Total number of Israeli startups that exited via acquisition (the startup was bought by another company). The bar chart shows acquisitions by exit year over the past 10 years.

startups:kpi-ipos.title: IPOs
startups:kpi-ipos.description: Total number of Israeli startups that exited via IPO (initial public offering on a public stock exchange). The bar chart shows IPOs by exit year over the past 10 years.

startups:kpi-time-to-exit.title: Time to Exit
startups:kpi-time-to-exit.description: Average number of years between founding year and exit year (acquisition or IPO) across all exited Israeli startups. The histogram shows the distribution of those exit times in 1-year buckets, capped at 30+ years.

startups:survival-distribution.title: Survival Distribution
startups:survival-distribution.description: Breakdown of all Israeli startups by current status: Active (still operating), Closed (shut down or dissolved), and Exit (acquired or went public). The center of the donut shows the total number of startups, or the value of the hovered slice.

startups:exit-funnel.title: Exit Funnel
startups:exit-funnel.description: How many Israeli startups make it through each progressive milestone toward an exit: All startups (everyone in the dataset), Funded (raised any disclosed capital), $1M+ (raised at least $1 million total), $10M+ (raised at least $10 million total), Exit (acquired or IPO). The percentage on each segment is the retention from the All-startups stage.

startups:time-to-exit.title: Time To Exit
startups:time-to-exit.description: Average and median number of years between founding year and exit year (acquisition or IPO) across all exited Israeli startups. The histogram below shows the distribution of those exit times in 1-year buckets, with the rightmost bucket capturing every exit at 30 or more years.

startups:industry-mix.title: Industry Share Over Time
startups:industry-mix.description: Stacked bar chart of the industry mix of Israeli startups by founding year. Each bar is one year, broken into 6 industry categories (IT and Telecom, Healthcare and Food, Consumer Goods and Services, Energy and Utilities, Financial Services, Media and Entertainment). Sub-industries that fall outside these 6 are bucketed into Unknown and excluded from the visible mix. The legend on the right shows each industry overall total and share across all years.

startups:signal-deeptech.title: Deeptech
startups:signal-deeptech.description: Share of Israeli startups flagged as deeptech. Deep tech companies require significant resources, long development cycles, specialized expertise, and substantial investment. The 10-segment meter visualizes that share. The number on the right is the absolute count of deeptech startups.

startups:signal-global.title: Operating Globally
startups:signal-global.description: Share of Israeli startups that have a product with a global audience. The 10-segment meter visualizes that share. The number on the right is the absolute count of globally operating startups.

startups:trending-up.title: Sub-Industries Trending Up By Share Of Total
startups:trending-up.description: Top 5 sub-industries whose share of new Israeli startups has grown the most over the past 10 founding years. Each line is one sub-industry. The value at year Y is the share of startups founded in year Y whose primary sub-industry falls in that group. The dashed tail marks the most recent year, which is still incomplete.

startups:trending-down.title: Sub-Industries Trending Down By Share Of Total
startups:trending-down.description: Top 5 sub-industries whose share of new Israeli startups has shrunk the most over the past 10 founding years. Each line is one sub-industry. The value at year Y is the share of startups founded in year Y whose primary sub-industry falls in that group. The dashed tail marks the most recent year, which is still incomplete.

startups:feed-funding.title: Funding Feed
startups:feed-funding.description: Latest funding rounds added to the dataset, gathered from multiple verifiable sources.

startups:feed-new-startups.title: New Startups
startups:feed-new-startups.description: Latest startups added to the dataset. Not necessarily new Startups founded.

startups:feed-notable.title: Notable Events
startups:feed-notable.description: Latest notable events gathered form news feeds, including acquisitions, IPOs, corporate events, strategic partnerships and more.

newStartups.description: Computational biology company providing algorithm-based tools for whole genome sequencing analysis and interpretation to link genetic variations to clinical conditions.
Developer of smart indoor cultivation systems that integrate advanced lighting and quality control measures to optimize plant growth.
A self-service marketplace platform that connects independent music artists with curators for promotion across streaming services, social media channels, and music blogs.
AI-enabled project delivery platform that integrates fragmented data sources to provide predictive insights and strategic decision-making tools for complex construction and capital programs.
A mobile application that provided crowdsourced information on the operating hours of various public and commercial locations. Users could contribute and verify data to ensure accuracy.
Digital automotive platform facilitating the online vehicle buying and leasing process.
A developer of mobile applications for major platforms, providing end-to-end product management, design, and development services, alongside a social application discovery tool.
A web-based marketplace connecting individuals seeking private event venues with homeowners willing to rent their space.
A comprehensive comparison and review platform for various online services, helping users make informed purchasing decisions.
Developing closed-loop sanitation systems that operate independently of urban infrastructure, treating wastewater to generate energy while maintaining self-cleaning capabilities.
Internet application that enables users to create online forms and surveys through simple text-based inputs.
A smart home aroma diffuser that allows users to mix multiple fragrance capsules simultaneously, controlled via a smartphone app or smart home hub.
Online platform facilitating real-time peer-to-peer support and knowledge exchange between IT professionals and technical specialists.

Event-based analytics and predictive machine learning platform that enriches user data and automates tasks for subscription-based businesses. It integrates with existing customer management and marketing systems to improve lead conversion and onboarding processes.
Social networking platform designed to enhance the podcast listening experience by enabling users to discover and connect with others who listen to the same content.
Digital media platform providing a service for purchasing and downloading high-quality movie files with integrated copyright protection.
Automated platform for mobile game publishers to identify and evaluate high-potential hyper-casual games through data-driven sorting and analysis.
Developer of mobile gaming applications designed to foster team engagement and enhance educational material internalization through collaborative problem-solving.
Developer of wearable technology for cyclists, featuring integrated safety signaling and connectivity features.
Platform enabling the integration of blockchain technology and tokenized economies into traditional gaming environments to facilitate value distribution among stakeholders.

notable.summary: Partnered with VodafoneThree to build an arrears support programme, resulting in a finalist nomination for Digital Initiative of the Year at the ICA Compliance Awards Europe 2026.
Launched a series of in-person workshops titled Embedding AI into Corporate Intelligence Workflows in Paris and Frankfurt.
AGEERA will showcase its advanced Energy Management System (EMS) at The smarter E Europe 2026 in Munich, Booth B0.355.
CEO Eran Ofir presented 'How do we want to implement AV? - A Scenario Discussion' at PAVE Europe's Automated Mobility in Practice course in Hamburg.
Sponsored the LUMABlanc event at Cannes Lions.
Hosted webinar 'Beyond Great Design – The Three Pillars of A/E Practice Profitability' in partnership with NLA, featuring speaker Lior Schnabel.
Attended senior-level networking event hosted by Article 25 at Kensington Palace.
Hosted first-ever NoviSign Americas Summit 2026 in Las Vegas one day before InfoComm.
Lasso hosted a webinar titled Forget AI Firewalls: Agentic Workflows Need Intent Security featuring Latio Tech.
Foresight Wins 2026 Datacloud Global Award for Best AI Innovation.
Stephen Ko presented on scaling AI with control, governance, and accountability at SuperAI Singapore.
Participated in the TAPA EMEA conference in Oberhausen to address supply chain security operations.
Mellie's Julie Stone presented at the Integrated Benefits Institute Atlanta Regional Event on The Multigenerational Workforce.
Launched agentic reporting tool to help MSPs generate customer security reports with conversational reporting experience.
Deeep Social is organizing the Creatives & Conversions TLV III conference at Tel Aviv University, featuring 10 speakers and 11 workshops.
Hosting the annual Architecture Next 2026 conference to discuss software architecture and AI trends.
Presented on the State of Agentic Guide 2026 at Infosecurity Europe 2026.
Mellie attended Insurtech Insights America in New York to connect with group benefit carriers.
Represented at the SIL Barcelona 2026 logistics industry event to discuss real-time visibility and reaction.
Participated in Infosecurity Europe 2026 at Excel London.
